essence: Creates drinks, atmosphere, and connection — blending craft, speed, and hospitality behind the bar.

identity.title: Bartender
identity.summary: Prepares and serves alcoholic and non‑alcoholic drinks, engages with customers, and maintains a welcoming, efficient bar environment.

salary.notes: Tips, cocktail specialisation, high‑end venues, and supervisory roles can raise earnings significantly, with experienced bartenders reaching £35k–£45k+.

qualifications: On‑the‑job training in bartending or hospitality
Knowledge of spirits, cocktails, and drink preparation
Understanding of licensing laws and responsible alcohol service

lifestyle.description: Bartenders work in lively, social environments where speed, multitasking, and hospitality are essential. Days (and often nights) involve mixing drinks, taking orders, managing queues, handling payments, cleaning equipment, and creating a friendly atmosphere. The role can be fast‑paced and physically demanding, especially during busy evenings or weekends. Despite the intensity, bartending is highly social and creative — bartenders become part of the venue’s personality and customer experience. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy people, rhythm, craft, and working in energetic, community‑driven spaces.

habits: Prepares cocktails with consistency and flair
Balances speed with accuracy during busy periods
